results.name: Victoria Palace
results.snippet: The Victory Palace is a stylised monumental classical design, with an arcaded ground level, a long colonnade of slim piers on the main front, and two recessed top floors.

results.name: Bellu Cemetery
results.snippet: Șerban Vodă Cemetery (commonly known as Bellu Cemetery) is the largest and most famous cemetery in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: National Museum of Romanian History

images.caption: The main view of the building
A portrait of Elisabeth of Wied in the museum
The Golden Helmet of Coţofeneşti, a Geto-Dacian helmet from the first half of the 4th century BC.

results.snippet: It's in a neoclassical late 19th-century building, and has exhibits documenting the evolution of society on Romania’s territory from the Paleolithic until today, a replica of Trajan’s Column in Rome and a very interesting collection of jewellery and bronze articles from Roman times up to the Kingdom of Romania.

results.name: Carol Parc Hotel
results.snippet: Boutique hotel looking onto Carol Park.

results.name: Stavropoleos Monastery
results.snippet: Built in the early 18th century, has some stunning decorative sculpture and amazing frescoes. A little jewel.

results.name: Cantacuzino Palace

images.caption: Detail of one of the gates
A smaller gate of the palace
Detail of that smaller gate of the palace
View of the palace, with its pair of Louis XIV style lions at the entrance
The glass and metal entrance of the palace
Handle of a door in the entrance room

results.snippet: It was built by architect Ion D. Berindey in the Beaux Arts style, having a few Rococo Revival rooms.

results.name: King Michael I Park

images.caption: Alley in Herăstrău Park in autumn
Monument to the Founders of the EU

results.snippet: The largest of several parks around man-made lakes on the Colentina River running through the city’s north and east side) houses the Village Museum, an open-air theater, various sports grounds, an amusement park and numerous restaurants and clubs.

results.name: Kretzulescu Church
results.snippet: An interesting example of the Brancovenesc style (1722).

results.name: Curtea Veche

images.caption: Curtea Veche (September 24, 2011) with the bust of Vlad Țepeș
Vlad Țepeș document establishing his court here on 20 September 1459

results.snippet: The ruins of the crown palace of the Wallachian princes, some parts dating as early as the 16th century.

results.name: Muzeul Țăranului Român

images.caption: The museum is built in Neo-Romanian style

results.snippet: Also dedicated to the traditional way of life, it focuses mainly on traditional interior decoration, tools, clothing and artifacts.

results.name: Cișmigiu Gardens

images.caption: Statue of Mihai Eminescu on Writers' Alley (Aleea Scriitorilor)

results.snippet: This a lovely small park; it's the oldest in the city (designed 1845-1860).

results.name: Tineretului Park
results.snippet: It has a large multipurpose building (Sala Polivalenta) used for concerts, sporting events, exhibitions, etc., an amusement park for children, boat-rental, several restaurants and bars.

results.name: Piața Unirii

images.caption: Fountains in the center of Unirii Square

results.snippet: Piața Unirii (; Unification Square or Union Square in English) is one of the largest squares in central Bucharest, located in the center of the city where Sectors 1, 2, 3, and 4 meet.

results.name: Romanian Athenaeum
results.snippet: A beautiful building is home of the George Enescu Philarmonic.

results.name: Revolution Square
results.snippet: Site of part of the Romanian Revolution of 1989.

results.name: Romanian Patriarchal Cathedral
results.snippet: Built in 1658. Next to it is the Mitropoliei Palace (1708) – the residence of the Orthodox Patriarch, a sort of small 'Romanian Vatican'.

results.name: Bucharest World Trade Center
results.snippet: The Bucharest World Trade Center is a business centre in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Radisson Blu Hotel
results.snippet: Upmarket place with indoor & outdoor pools, 5 restaurants and 4 bars.

results.name: JW Marriott Bucharest Grand
results.snippet: Large hotel behind the Parliament building. 4 restaurants, bar, spa & sauna.

results.name: National Military Museum
results.snippet: Has collections of weapons dating since the prehistoric times and permanent exhibitions dedicated to important military events, including the Romanian revolution of 1989, and an outdoor exhibit of relatively modern weaponry, including cannons, tanks, helicopters.

results.name: Mormântul Ostașului Necunoscut

images.caption: The Tomb next to the Communist mausoleum

results.snippet: The Tomb of the Unknown Soldier (Romanian. Mormântul Soldatului Necunoscut) is a monument located in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Bamboo
results.snippet: It's the largest club in Bucharest. Upmarket and expensive.

results.name: Palace of the Patriarchate

images.caption: Commemorative plaque located on the palace façade. It reads: "In the old assembly building on this site the electoral assembly met under popular pressure in 1859, electing Alexandru Ion Cuza prince of Wallachia on 24 January and achieving union in this way."

results.snippet: The Palace of the Chamber of Deputies (Romanian.

results.name: Crețulescu Palace

images.caption: The Krețulescu Palace on a Romanian postage stamp from 2014

results.snippet: From 1972 to 2011, the Crețulescu Palace housed the headquarters of UNESCO's European Centre for Higher Education UNESCO-CEPES (known as CEPES after its French name, Centre Europeén pour l'enseignement supérieur).

results.name: Crowne Plaza
results.snippet: Business hotel close to Romexpo grounds.

results.name: Caru' cu Bere
results.snippet: One of the most famous restaurants in Bucharest, in a historic building. Student menu for 14,50 Lei!

results.name: Băneasa Shopping City
results.snippet: Opened in April 2008.

results.name: Zoo Băneasa
results.snippet: The Zoo, with a total area of 5.85 ha, is a public facility that maintains and exposes live, wild, indigenous and exotic collections of animals to achieve two main goals. preservation of fauna (with priority to endangered species); and training, education and recreation of the visitor audience.

results.snippet: The Palace of Justice (Romanian.

results.name: Grigore Antipa National Museum of Natural History

images.caption: The worldwide only intact skeleton of a Deinotherium Gigantissimum

results.snippet: This has over 300,000 exhibits illustrating the transformations of Earth and the evolution of species.

results.name: Epoque
results.snippet: 5-star all-suites hotel in a quiet location just west of Cișmigiu Gardens.

results.name: Radu Vodă Monastery

images.caption: Church of the Radu Vodă Monastery

results.snippet: Radu Vodă Monastery is a Romanian Orthodox monastery in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Mihai Vodă Monastery

images.caption: The bridge over the river Dâmbovița, getting to the Mihai Vodă Monastery (William Watts, following the drawing from 1793 of Luigi Mayer)

attribution.attribution_text: Original uploader was Pixi at ro.wikipedia
Original uploader was Pixi at ro.wikipedia

results.snippet: The monastery was an important archeological site; inside the monastery yard used to be a Dacian archeological site, more than 3000 years old, where old pottery and other relics were found.

results.name: Palace of the National Military Circle

attribution.attribution_text: The original uploader was Pixi at Romanian Wikipedia.

results.snippet: The Palace of the National Military Circle, also known as the Officers' Circle Palace (Romanian:Cercul Militar Național) is located on Constantin Mile street in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Antim Monastery

images.caption: The Bell Tower and Entrance of the Monastery
Detail of an outside column in front of the Church
Door and inscription of the Church

results.snippet: An Orthodox monastery in Brâncovenesc style from 1715 with a church, a museum and a shop. The church, museum and shop are open to the public.

results.name: AFI Cotroceni
results.snippet: AFI Cotroceni is a shopping mall in Bucharest, Romania.

results.name: Dimitrie Leonida Technical Museum

images.caption: One of the rare museums, where government approval is needed to take pictures

results.snippet: The Romanian Technical Museum was founded in 1909 by Dimitrie Leonida, inspired by the München Technical Museum, he had visited during his studies in Charlottenburg Polytechnic Institute.

results.name: Equestrian statue of Carol I

images.caption: The Equestrian statue of Carol I, 2015
The new statue of King Carol I, a work by Florin Codre. The façade behind it is the library of "Carol I University Foundation"

results.snippet: The statue is located on Calea Victoriei and stands in Piața Palatului (a square that was renamed "The Revolution Square" after the bloody events of 1989, opposite the National Museum of Art of Romania (the former royal palace) and in front of the Central University Library, Bucharest.

results.name: Dealul Mitropoliei

images.caption: Bucharest at the beginning of the 19th century. Dealul Mitropoliei is in the centre of the drawing; Curtea Arsă to the left and Radu Vodă Monastery to the right (drawing by R. Bielitz)

results.snippet: Dealul Mitropoliei (, Metropolitanate Hill), also called Dealul Patriarhiei (, Patriarchate Hill), is a small hill in Bucharest, Romania and an important historic, cultural, architectural, religious and touristic point in the national capital.

results.name: Pasajul Macca-Vilacrosse
results.snippet: Today it hosts several indoor/outdoor eating establishments, including an Egyptian-themed bar/restaurant, the Blues Cafe, a bistro, a Chinese restaurant and a wine bar.

results.name: Lacul Lebedelor
results.snippet: Lacul Lebedelor ("Swan Lake") is an artificial lake in the center of Bucharest, Sector 1, in Cișmigiu Gardens, close to Lake Cișmigiu.

results.name: Chiajna Monastery
results.snippet: Chiajna Monastery is the name of a ruined church situated on the outskirts of Bucharest, Romania which is the subject of many legends, including the story that it is cursed.

results.name: People's Salvation Cathedral
results.snippet: The People's Salvation Cathedral will have the world's biggest collection of church mosaic (interior decoration) when it is completed, having about 25,000 square meters, including the mosaic of the altar is about 3,000 square meters.
